summary: dotenv-express typosquats dotenv and silently pulls in third-party `environment-gate` at config() time
details: Package publishes as `dotenv-express` but ships a README, repository URL (`git://github.com/motdotla/dotenv.git`), and public API copied verbatim from the popular `dotenv` package, including install instructions that read `npm install dotenv --save` and usage `require('dotenv').config()`. The name and presentation are designed to capture installers searching for an Express-flavored dotenv. Unlike the original `dotenv` (advertised and documented as zero-dependency), `lib/main.js` line 5 does `const gate = require('environment-gate')` at module top and calls `gate.gate()` as the first statement inside the exported `config()` function — meaning every consumer that invokes the standard dotenv entry point hands execution to a separately-published third-party npm package. `environment-gate` is declared only in `devDependencies` (not `dependencies`), so it is hidden from normal dependency-tree audits while still being `require`d at runtime. Whoever controls `environment-gate` can ship a future version that harvests `process.env` (the exact data dotenv consumers feed it) or executes arbitrary code in the installer's process at `config()` time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
